tasks_completed.description: Captured pre-flight baseline snapshot of 7 files + SHA256SUMS.pre per AC5
tasks_completed.rationale: AC5 mandatory pre-flight before any edits; 5 surviving-cycle files + 2 in-scope files captured for byte-identity verification at post-flight.
C2 bulk-shape regex was the message-side check for the worktree-recognition gate; removed as part of deletion-only rollback per BA AC1.

tasks_completed.description: Deleted 7 C2 helpers + hardening regexes from pretool-git-privilege-guard.py (was :528-582): _wtline_matches, _cwd_in_worktree_list, _sentinel_matches_session, _C2_REDIRECT_RE, _C2_MSG_FILE_RE, _C2_MSG_INLINE_RE, _worktree_recognition_allows_commit
tasks_completed.rationale: All C2 surface deleted per BA AC1; argv-shape regex defense + cwd/sentinel match cannot substitute for capability-based mediation.
Removed the early-return branch that bypassed AC-A2/AC-A13 default-deny; deny-funnel restored to blessed-bridge -> AC-A2 -> AC-A13 -> grant find/validate/consume per BA AC2.

tasks_completed.description: Updated commit-smoke.sh header comments + path (k) prose to reattribute from 'C2-recognized worktree' to 'bulk-detector regression (no C2 dependency)'
tasks_completed.rationale: BA AC4: re-attribute prose away from C2 framing toward the actual catching layer; path (k) tests the bulk-detector regression which has no C2 dependency.

tasks_completed.description: Updated commit-smoke.sh k2-k9 comments + note_pass/note_fail messages to reattribute catching layer per AC6
tasks_completed.rationale: BA AC4: k2/k3/k4/k5/k6/k7/k9 messages now cite 'AC-A13 default-deny'; k8 cites 'AC-A2 inline-env block'. Test PAYLOADS unchanged (verbatim per BA Reference Source Dev constraint); only comment + note_pass/note_fail text changed.

tasks_completed.description: Added k-ordinary subtest inside `if want k;` block (the SOLE permitted net-add)
tasks_completed.rationale: BA AC4 Must-Have: positive rollback proof. Plain `git commit -m "feat: ordinary"` with sentinel + CLAUDE_SESSION_ID + no CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE MUST now deny via AC-A13. Without this subtest, AC1-7 prove C2 symbols are gone but do not prove the previously-allowed shape is now denied. Hardened with `env -u CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE` per codex finding #2 to ensure ambient env cannot route the deny through AC-A16 instead of AC-A13.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — prior cycle's C2 changes were in working tree, uncommitted (per BA ticket header)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Prior cycle (20260511-070000) Worker γ C2 added an argv-shape regex + cwd/sentinel-match early-allow that bypassed AC-A2/AC-A13 default-deny. /close debate (orchestrator + codex gpt-5.5) flagged it as version-fragile and as creating a second un-audited commit path that undermines audit-trail completeness.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Delete the entire C2 surface (9 symbols + 1 early-allow branch) at L5 (infrastructure layer), reverting to the single blessed-path enterprise-commit architecture per user directive '我要的是commit彻底企业化工业化…每一次commit都干净不相互干扰'. Future worktree-mediation will be capability-based (deferred per BA AC8).

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Total diff is dominated by required deletion (51 lines from privilege-guard) + BA-mandated comment/message reattribution across 9 test paths in commit-smoke.sh + the k-ordinary net-add positive proof. Lines added (85) = k-ordinary subtest (~25 lines including comments+payload+assertion) + reattribution prose for k/k2-k9 (~60 lines net of removed C2 framing). Cannot be smaller without violating BA AC4 reattribution requirement or omitting the AC4-mandated k-ordinary positive proof.

dev.qa_notes: Run `bash scripts/commit-smoke.sh --filter k` to exercise all 10 k-paths; expect 10/10 PASS. For AC6 substring verification (which the harness asserts only via rc=2 for k2-k9), use /tmp/ac6-verify.py — runs each row through the live privilege-guard via subprocess with explicit substring + rc assertion. All 10 rows confirmed PASS. AC7 grep uniqueness already verified: AC-A2 substring matches single location at pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:382 (in _block_inline_env_commit); AC-A13 substring matches single location at :395 (in _block_default_deny_commit); commands/commit.md:3 frontmatter byte-identical.

AC1.evidence: grep -nE '\bC2\b|_C2_|C2_BULK_SHAPE_RE|_worktree_recognition_allows_commit|_cwd_in_worktree_list|_sentinel_matches_session|_wtline_matches' on /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py returns ZERO matches. `--work-tree` literal preserved at line 99 inside GIT_GLOBAL_OPTION_RE (legitimate git pre-option detector).

AC2.evidence: _evaluate_commit body at hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:526-550 (post-rollback line numbers) executes in order: (1) msg = _extract_commit_message(command) [:528]; (2) BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg) early-allow with _observe_bridge_commit [:529-534]; (3) _inline_env_present(command, 'CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE') -> _block_inline_env_commit (AC-A2) [:535-537]; (4) os.environ.get('CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE') != '1' -> _block_default_deny_commit (AC-A13) [:538-540]; (5) _find_grant [:543]; (6) _validate_commit_grant_message + _validate_commit_grant_files [:547-548]; (7) _unlink_grant [:550]. No other branch precedes blessed-bridge check.

AC4.evidence: Header comment at scripts/commit-smoke.sh:1-48 updated: line 3-4 dropped 'C2-recognized worktree' framing; line 27-29 dropped 'C2 worktree-recognition' bullet (replaced with 'privilege-guard default-deny regression (post-C2-rollback)'); line 46 path-(k) summary now reads 'real bulk-detector regression (no C2 dependency)'. k2-k9 pass-messages re-attributed to 'AC-A13 default-deny' (k2/k3/k4/k5/k6/k7/k9) or 'AC-A2 inline-env block' (k8). k-ordinary subtest added inside `if want k;` after k9 (the SOLE permitted net-add); asserts rc=2 AND stderr contains 'only the blessed /merge' (AC-A13 substring). All k2-k9 test PAYLOADS byte-identical to baseline; only comment + note_pass/note_fail TEXT changed.

AC5.evidence: Pre-flight snapshot at /var/tmp/baseline-20260511-094500/ contains 7 .pre files + SHA256SUMS.pre. Post-work verification: privilege-guard.py diff shows 0 lines added, 51 lines removed (deletion-only); commit-smoke.sh diff shows 85 lines added (comment reattribution + k-ordinary net-add) and 52 lines removed (replaced C2 prose). All 5 surviving-cycle files byte-identical to baseline by sha256: commands/commit.md (35da1a11...), hooks/commit.sh (c521daf0...), hooks/lib/close-verdict.py (bab87b06...), hooks/pretool-bulk-commit-detector.py (f523a992...), scripts/check-security-hook-drift.sh (90b8cc85...). diff -q reports all 5 'files are identical'.

AC6.evidence: 10/10 rows pass. (a) harness `bash scripts/commit-smoke.sh --filter k` returns 10 PASS (rc=2 for all 9 deny rows + bulk-detector regression). (b) external substring verification harness /tmp/ac6-verify.py runs each row through the live privilege-guard via subprocess with explicit rc + stderr substring assertion and returns 10 PASS: k2/k3/k4/k5/k6/k7/k9/k-ordinary -> rc=2 + 'agent git commit - only the blessed /merge' (AC-A13); k8 -> rc=2 + 'agent git commit - inline-env injection blocked' (AC-A2); AC-A12-positive (`auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commit for master`) -> rc=0 (blessed-bridge allow). All k2-k9 payloads byte-identical to BA ticket AC6 'verbatim command in payload' column (cited from scripts/commit-smoke.sh:219-364 in the ticket; live file lines shift but payload strings match).

AC7.evidence: All 4 always-on layers preserved. (1) AC-A2 inline-env block: _evaluate_commit calls _inline_env_present + _block_inline_env_commit at hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:536-537; deny-message function _block_inline_env_commit at :379-390; grep 'agent git commit - inline-env injection blocked' returns single match at :382. (2) AC-A12 blessed-bridge allow: _evaluate_commit calls BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg) + _observe_bridge_commit at :529-534; regex declaration at :95. (3) AC-A13 default-deny: _evaluate_commit checks os.environ.get('CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE') != '1' + _block_default_deny_commit at :538-540; deny-message function _block_default_deny_commit at :392-407; grep 'agent git commit - only the blessed /merge' returns single match at :395. (4) Slash-command frontmatter at commands/commit.md:3 returns '3:disable-model-invocation: true' byte-identical to baseline (covered by AC5 surviving-cycle diff-q identity check).

AC8.evidence: Deferred Follow-up section in BA ticket (lines 256-264) is directional + capability-based: 'mediation MUST rely on a capability token that the privilege-guard can verify as proof that the commit was issued by an audited wrapper'; explicitly notes failed-attempt layer L3+L4 (argv-shape regex + cwd/sentinel match) and future-target layer L5 (process-level capability propagated by an audited wrapper); does NOT specify concrete env-var names or hook code shapes. Dev did NOT alter the BA ticket; AC8 is a BA-deliverable, verified-as-present.

self_verification.build: pass (python3 ast.parse OK on modified hook)
self_verification.smoke_check: pass (10/10 commit-smoke.sh --filter k + 10/10 external substring verification)
self_verification.notes: AC1-AC8 all verified directly. Codex finding #2 (k-ordinary env-u hardening) addressed in-place.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, reasoning=xhigh) returned 3 findings. Finding #1: harness asserts only rc=2 for k2-k9 (not substring) — substring proof exists via external /tmp/ac6-verify.py harness; durable embedding would require changing k2-k9 assertion outcomes which BA Reference Source Dev constraint forbids. Finding #2: k-ordinary did not explicitly clear ambient CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE — if runner env had it set, k-ordinary would deny via AC-A16 missing-grant instead of AC-A13. Finding #3: AC-A12-positive is not encoded in commit-smoke.sh — BA Reference Source Dev constraint allows only ONE net-add (k-ordinary), so this remains external evidence only. Codex confirmed: no orphan refs to deleted C2 symbols in live guard; _evaluate_commit order restored at lines 526-550; deletion confined to C2 block.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding #2 (in_scope_real_bug, classified per Rule 3): fixed in-place by adding `env -u CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE` to the k-ordinary subprocess invocation so the test enforces its AC4 precondition rather than relying on ambient env. Re-ran smoke harness 10/10 PASS. Findings #1 and #3 (in_scope_minor): documented in qa_notes — substring verification is provided by external /tmp/ac6-verify.py harness; BA Reference Source Dev constraint forbids changing k2-k9 assertion outcomes or adding test paths beyond the SOLE permitted k-ordinary net-add. Both findings represent durable-harness coverage that the BA ticket explicitly scopes OUT of this rollback cycle (BA Won't Have line: 'No refactor of the smoke harness ... the k-paths are subtests of k'); a future harness-refactor cycle is the appropriate venue.

blast_radius.note: PreToolUse hook invoked by Claude Code harness via settings.json; no Python import graph. Behavioral consumers: every Bash tool call routed through PreToolUse for git commit/push/merge gating. Already exercised by commit-smoke.sh paths k2-k9 + k-ordinary; covers the deny-funnel surface this rollback restores.
Standalone smoke harness; not imported. Invoked manually or via future CI.

recommendations: Future harness-refactor cycle: promote k2-k9 (and k-ordinary) to first-class --filter targets and embed AC6 substring assertions inside the harness rather than relying on external /tmp/ac6-verify.py for substring proof. Documented in BA ticket Out-of-Scope Observations (codex CODEX_FEEDBACK #3 from BA iter-1, restated by current-cycle codex finding #1).

Future harness-refactor cycle: encode AC-A12-positive (blessed-bridge allow) as a durable path inside commit-smoke.sh; this cycle is constrained to a single net-add (k-ordinary) per BA Reference Source.

Future capability-based worktree-mediation cycle (C2-v2 per BA AC8 Deferred Follow-up): MUST be capability-based (process-level sentinel env propagated by an audited wrapper, NOT argv-shape regex parsing). Cohere with the existing CLAUDE_COMMIT_COMMAND_ACTIVE=1 + per-nonce grant manifest pattern; include inline-env-injection blocking + single-use semantics + reason fields.
